content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Accepted the port-abstraction leak, stale-run provenance issue, and journal compatibility risk. Rejected the dedup-key amendment because the current reconstruction helper is already bead-scoped, so omitting active_bead_id is redundant in present behavior.

## Votes

- `fr-13-3846db7d`: Accept
  load_planned_elsewhere_mappings lives behind the service-layer port boundary, but its actual read path bypasses MilestoneJournalPort and directly instantiates FsMilestoneJournalStore. That is not just stylistic: a non-filesystem or test journal implementation can be used for writes yet can never be used for this read, so the abstraction cannot round-trip correctly and alternative adapters would silently read the wrong backing store. The unused PlannedElsewhereMappingPort parameter also shows the API is wired to the wrong dependency. The read path should accept and use a journal port.
- `fr-13-b76b3832`: Reject
  The identity mismatch is real in the abstract, but the claimed bug is not reachable in the current code path. reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings is called with existing_mappings already filtered to one active bead, and every reconstructed mapping is created with active_bead_id set from that same bead_id argument. Under that bead-scoped contract, (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) and (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) are equivalent. Using the 3-tuple would be harmless cleanup, but this amendment overstates it as a present correctness defect.
- `fr-13-9cfa6efb`: Accept
  This is a real correctness issue. Planned-elsewhere mappings are persisted before it is known which run attempt will be the visible successful one, but the persisted mapping carries no run or attempt provenance. Later, verify_planned_elsewhere_after_success reloads mappings by milestone and filters only by active_bead_id before verifying and commenting unverified mappings. The run_id filtering in reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings only applies to reconstructed aggregates; it does not exclude stale mappings already present in the journal. Because journal rebuild collapses only exact (active_bead_id, finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) duplicates, distinct stale mappings from an abandoned or superseded run can survive and be re-verified/commented during a later successful run for the same bead.
- `fr-13-07fe0b4d`: Accept
  This is a real persistence-compatibility hazard. After the new planned_elsewhere_mapped event is written, an older checkout that does not know that MilestoneEventType variant will fail to deserialize the journal because the enum has no unknown fallback and read_journal_from_path converts the serde failure into CorruptRecord. That means a rollback or downgrade to pre-feature code can become unable to read journal.ndjson for affected milestones. A backward-compatible read path, an unknown-event fallback, or an explicit one-way migration policy is needed before emitting the new event.
